meanings.definition: Dirty, shabby, unpleasant, or morally questionable in appearance, behavior, or character.
examples: They stayed in a sleazy hotel near the station.
The businessman was known for his sleazy deals.
She disliked the sleazy atmosphere of the nightclub.
His sleazily written advertisements fooled many customers.

meanings.definition: Dishonest, immoral, or lacking respectability, especially in a cheap or vulgar way.
examples: The movie portrayed a sleazy politician hungry for power.
He worked for a sleazier company before changing jobs.
People criticized the channel for its sleazy content.
The reporter exposed several sleazy business practices.

meanings.definition: An extra amount of money added to the usual price, cost, or charge.
examples: The airline added a fuel surcharge to the ticket price.
Customers complained about the unexpected surcharges on their bills.
A weekend surcharge was applied at the restaurant.
The delivery company increased its holiday surcharge this year.

meanings.definition: To add an extra charge or fee to the normal cost of something.
examples: The bank surcharged customers for late payments.
Some stores surcharge credit card purchases.
The hotel was surcharging guests during the festival season.
They surcharged the shipment because of its extra weight.
